heading: Imposition of tax
label: 4475 Imposition of tax
notes: Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 119–21, title VII, § 70604(c)139 Stat. 286July 4, 2025“The amendments made by this section [enacting this subchapter] shall apply to transfers made after .”December 31, 2025
amendments: Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 119–21, title VII, § 70604(c)139 Stat. 286July 4, 2025“The amendments made by this section [enacting this subchapter] shall apply to transfers made after .”December 31, 2025

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: There is hereby imposed on any remittance transfer a tax equal to 1 percent of the amount of such transfer.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The tax imposed by this section with respect to any remittance transfer shall be paid by the sender with respect to such transfer.

children.heading: Collection of tax
children.label: 2 Collection of tax
children.text: The remittance transfer provider with respect to any remittance transfer shall collect the amount of the tax imposed under subsection (a) with respect to such transfer from the sender and remit such tax quarterly to the Secretary at such time and in such manner as provided by the Secretary,1So in original. The comma probably should be a period.1

children.heading: Secondary liability
children.label: 3 Secondary liability
children.text: Where any tax imposed by subsection (a) is not paid at the time the transfer is made, then to the extent that such tax is not collected, such tax shall be paid by the remittance transfer provider.

children.heading: Tax limited to cash and similar instruments
children.label: c Tax limited to cash and similar instruments
children.text: The tax imposed under subsection (a) shall apply only to any remittance transfer for which the sender provides cash, a money order, a cashier’s check, or any other similar physical instrument (as determined by the Secretary) to the remittance transfer provider.

children.heading: Nonapplication to certain noncash remittance transfers
children.label: d Nonapplication to certain noncash remittance transfers
A
children.text: which is described in subparagraphs (A) through (H) of , United States Code, andsection 5312(a)(2) of title 31

children.label: B
children.text: that is subject to the requirements under subchapter II of chapter 53 of such title, or
funded with a debit card or a credit card which is issued in the United States.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The terms “remittance transfer”, “remittance transfer provider”, and “sender” shall each have the respective meanings given such terms by section 919(g) of the Electronic Fund Transfer Act (–1(g)).15 U.S.C. 1693o

children.heading: Credit card
children.label: 2 Credit card
children.text: The term “credit card” has the same meaning given such term under section 920(c)(3) of the Electronic Fund Transfer Act (–2(c)(3)).15 U.S.C. 1693o

children.heading: Debit card
children.label: 3 Debit card
children.text: The term “debit card” has the same meaning given such term under section 920(c)(2) of the Electronic Fund Transfer Act (–2(c)(2)), without regard to subparagraph (B) of such section.15 U.S.C. 1693o

children.heading: Application of anti-conduit rules
children.label: f Application of anti-conduit rules
children.text: For purposes of section 7701(), with respect to any multiple-party arrangements involving the sender, a remittance transfer shall be treated as a financing transaction.l
